tekst: After THAT intro, we are absolutely cooking — welcome back to the pod, people! It is matchday chaos, the World Cup is in full swing, and I am BUZZING!
Buzzing is one word for it. Clueless is another. But welcome everyone, let's get into it — and what a batch of results to dig through.
Okay so Germany seven, Curaçao one. SEVEN. I nearly fell off my couch. And I read that like twenty-five of Curaçao's twenty-six players weren't even born in the country! Wild!

Yes, Liam, it's called eligibility rules. Perfectly legal. Kai Havertz is up to two goals on the tournament and Germany look absolutely menacing. Whoever in our competition backed Germany to win that one comfortably — smart call.
Sweden also went off — five-one against Tunisia! And Yasin Ayari with two goals, though apparently he didn't even celebrate at first? The man was saving the gunpowder!
Composed. Professional. Unlike a certain co-host who probably screamed at his television when the USA beat Paraguay.

I did NOT scream. I merely expressed myself at a very high volume for an extended period. Balogun with two goals, Mitch! TWO! The headline literally called it the best performance in US World Cup history!

Yes yes, USA top of Group D, very good. Now — the competition standings. Simon is sitting at the top on seven points, which is frankly suspicious. Tobias and Jonas are joint second on six, breathing right down his neck.
And then we have this giant blob of five-pointers — André, Marius, Håkon, Bjørn, and Arya. Five people on five points! That is an absolute traffic jam. One good round and anyone could jump the table.

Simon needs to watch himself. One upset and this thing completely reshuffles. And speaking of upsets — Netherlands drawing two-two with Japan, Scotland beating Haiti, and Ivory Coast nicking one-nil against Ecuador. Anyone who saw those coming deserves a medal.

Okay, coming up next — and I need everyone to know I am barely keeping it together — Norway play Iraq in Group I! Strand Larsen is apparently in great form after a tough few months personally, and the national coach is being shielded from media pressure before the game. Sounds like they're protecting him!

It's called man-management, Liam. And yes, Jørgen Strand Larsen is apparently back to his best — the Norwegian press say Haaland and Strand Larsen are both fit and ready. If France top Group I and Norway are second, this could set up a very tasty knockout bracket.

France versus Senegal is also on the slate — that is a HUGE game. Meanwhile Spain face Cape Verde and Belgium take on Egypt. Arya, Håkon, Bjørn — whoever you lot picked for these matches, now is the time to deliver!
And if Tobias and Jonas have backed France and Spain to win, which seems the sensible call, they could leapfrog Simon entirely. Simon, your lead is on a very thin thread, mate.

Also — massive geopolitical news this week, a deal between the USA and Iran was announced, which is obviously huge. And somehow a so-called MAGA cruise ship ended up in Trondheim with protesters. The World Cup really does bring everything together.

Only you could connect a cruise ship protest to a football podcast, Liam. Right — before this goes completely off the rails, final thought: Norway versus Iraq, Strand Larsen fit, Haaland ready, the competition is razor tight. This next round will separate the wolves from the people who just guessed randomly — Bjørn, I'm looking at you.
It's all to play for, the competition is on fire, and we will be back with full reaction after the matches! Don't touch that dial — Hit the outro!
